2603.18750.title: Automatic detection of Gen-AI texts: A comparative framework of neural models
2603.18750.paper_summary: The rapid proliferation of Large Language Models has significantly increased the difficulty of distinguishing between human-written and AI generated texts, raising critical issues across academic, editorial, and social domains. This paper investigates the problem of AI generated text detection through the design, implementation, and comparative evaluation of multiple machine learning based detectors. Four neural architectures are developed and analyzed: a Multilayer Perceptron, a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network, a MobileNet-based CNN, and a Transformer model. The proposed models are benchmarked against widely used online detectors, including ZeroGPT, GPTZero, QuillBot, Originality.AI, Sapling, IsGen, Rephrase, and Writer. Experiments are conducted on the COLING Multilingual Dataset, considering both English and Italian configurations, as well as on an original thematic dataset focused on Art and Mental Health. Results show that supervised detectors achieve more stable and robust performance than commercial tools across different languages and domains, highlighting key strengths and limitations of current detection strategies.
2603.18750.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型的快速增長顯著提高了區分人類撰寫文本與AI生成文本的難度，並在學術、編輯和社會領域引發了關鍵問題。本文通過設計、實施和比較評估多個基於機器學習的檢測器，研究了AI生成文本檢測的問題。開發並分析了四種神經架構：多層感知器、一維卷積神經網絡、基於MobileNet的CNN和Transformer模型。所提出的模型與廣泛使用的在線檢測器進行基準測試，包括ZeroGPT、GPTZero、QuillBot、Originality.AI、Sapling、IsGen、Rephrase和Writer。實驗在COLING多語言數據集上進行，考慮了英語和意大利語的配置，以及一個專注於藝術和心理健康的原創主題數據集。結果顯示，監督式檢測器在不同語言和領域中比商業工具實現了更穩定和更強大的性能，突顯了當前檢測策略的主要優勢和局限性。
2603.18750.author: Cristian Buttaro et.al.
